field.name: source_path
field.desc: Original source code path for cross-validation

block.field: DO NOT ask user "Should I continue?" or "How would you like to proceed?" between steps
DO NOT offer options like "Full execution / Sample execution / Pause" — always execute ALL steps to completion
DO NOT suggest "Let me split this into batches" or "Let's do this in parts" — process ALL items sequentially
DO NOT pause to list what you plan to do next — just do it
DO NOT ask for confirmation before generating output files
ONLY pause at explicit <event action="confirm"> blocks defined in the workflow
FORBIDDEN: Python, Ruby, Perl, compiled binaries, or any runtime requiring separate installation
PERMITTED scripting: PowerShell (Windows) and Bash (Linux/Mac) ONLY
PERMITTED Node.js: ONLY for existing project scripts
For JSON validation use: node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('file.json','utf8'))"
DO NOT create temporary .py, .rb, .pl, .bat files — use inline commands via run_in_terminal

block.desc: Completion marker validation — MUST verify before writing done marker
block.field: MUST verify quality-report.json exists before writing quality-done.json marker
NEVER write completion marker with missing quality report
Read all generated documentation files in the platform directory
INDEX.md, tech-stack.md, architecture.md, conventions-design.md, conventions-dev.md, conventions-unit-test.md, conventions-system-test.md, conventions-build.md
conventions-data.md (backend OR has_data_layer), ui-style/ui-style-guide.md (frontend)
{platform_id}.analysis.json
All files found, File sizes
Verify all expected documents and sections exist
All platforms require: INDEX.md, tech-stack.md, architecture.md, conventions-design.md, conventions-dev.md, conventions-unit-test.md, conventions-system-test.md, conventions-build.md
Backend platforms additionally require: conventions-data.md
Web/mobile/desktop platforms additionally require: ui-style/ui-style-guide.md
INDEX.md must contain: Platform summary, Technology stack overview, Navigation links to all convention documents, Agent usage guide
tech-stack.md must contain: Overview section, Core Technologies table, Dependencies section (grouped by category), Development Tools section, Configuration Files list

architecture.md must contain: Component/Module Architecture section, State Management / Dependency Injection section, API Integration / Module Organization section, Styling Approach / Middleware section
conventions-dev.md must contain: Naming Conventions section, Directory Structure section, Code Style section, Import/Export Patterns section, Pre-Development Checklist section
conventions-build.md must contain: Build Tool & Configuration section, Environment Management section, Build Profiles & Outputs section
{platform_id}.analysis.json must contain: platform_id field, platform_type field, analyzed_at timestamp, topics object with all expected topics, documents_generated array, coverage_summary object
Completeness check: ${completeness_result.documents_found}/${completeness_result.documents_expected} documents, ${completeness_result.sections_passed}/${completeness_result.sections_checked} sections
Verify information is consistent across documents and with source code
Read {source_path}/package.json
Framework versions match documentation
Key dependency versions match documentation
Build tool versions match documentation
Each dependency in tech-stack.md exists in package.json
Version format is consistent
INDEX.md links to all other documents
conventions-design.md references ui-style/ui-style-guide.md (for frontend)
All documents have consistent platform_id references
All documents have consistent platform_type references
ESLint config path → file exists
Prettier config path → file exists
Build config path → file exists

Cross-validation: ${cross_validation_result.version_checks.passed}/${cross_validation_result.version_checks.total} version checks, ${cross_validation_result.reference_checks.passed}/${cross_validation_result.reference_checks.total} reference checks
Verify naming conventions and style are uniform across all documents
Component naming (PascalCase vs camelCase) matches conventions-dev.md
File naming conventions match conventions-dev.md
Variable naming conventions match conventions-dev.md
Platform identifier is consistent (e.g., web-react vs react-web)
Framework name is consistent (e.g., React vs react)
Language name is consistent (e.g., TypeScript vs Typescript)
Quote style matches conventions-dev.md (single vs double)
Semicolon usage matches conventions-dev.md
Indentation style matches conventions-dev.md

block.desc: Check UI reference for frontend platforms
block.field: conventions-design.md contains reference to ui-style/ui-style-guide.md
ui_style_analysis_level indicator exists
Consistency: ${consistency_result.status} - ${consistency_result.issues.length} issues found
Verify all documents properly cite their sources
File reference block exists at document beginning
Contains list of referenced files
File paths use relative paths (NOT absolute or file://)
**Diagram Source** annotation exists after diagram
Source file path is provided
Path uses relative format
**Section Source** annotation exists at section end OR generic guidance note is present
No paths start with drive letter (e.g., d:/, C:\)
No paths use file:// protocol
Correct relative depth used (e.g., ../../../../ for 4 levels)

Source Traceability: ${traceability_result.documents_with_cite_block}/${traceability_result.documents_total} documents with cite blocks, ${traceability_result.absolute_paths_found} absolute paths, ${traceability_result.file_protocol_found} file:// protocols
Verify all Mermaid diagrams are compatible with standard rendering
No `style` definitions
No `direction` keyword

No HTML tags (e.g.,
,
)
No nested subgraphs
Valid diagram type declaration
Properly closed brackets
Valid node syntax
Use graph TB/LR for structure diagrams
Use flowchart TD for process diagrams
Use sequenceDiagram for API diagrams
Mermaid Compatibility: ${mermaid_result.diagrams_passed}/${mermaid_result.diagrams_checked} diagrams passed, ${mermaid_result.issues.length} issues found
Generate comprehensive quality report in JSON format
{platform_dir}/quality-report.json
quality_score = (passed / total_checks) * 100; Warnings count as 0.5 towards failed; Critical failures (missing required documents) deduct 10 points each
Create completion marker file to signal quality check completion
MUST verify quality-report.json exists before writing marker
{platform_dir}/quality-done.json
{ "platform_id": "{platform_id}", "status": "completed", "quality_score": {calculated_score}, "report_path": "quality-report.json", "completed_at": "{ISO 8601 timestamp}" }
completed: Quality check finished successfully; failed: Critical error during quality check
total_checks: ${quality_report.summary.total_checks}, passed: ${quality_report.summary.passed}, warnings: ${quality_report.summary.warnings}, failed: ${quality_report.summary.failed}
Platform directory not found: {platform_dir}
Required document missing: {missing_document}
Analysis report missing, skipping cross-validation with source
Mermaid parsing error in {diagram_location}
